stories.headline: Meta's AI turns brain waves into text without surgery
stories.blurb: Meta built Brain2Qwerty, an AI system that reads brain activity through a cap with electrodes and types what someone is trying to say. Early tests show people can 'type' around 30 characters per minute just by thinking about moving their hands to type — no implant required.
stories.why_it_matters: First real alternative to Neuralink's invasive approach for people who can't speak or move.
stories.original_headline: From Brain Waves to Words: Brain2Qwerty Offers a New Path to Communication Without Surgery

stories.headline: OpenAI is building its own chip to run AI models
stories.blurb: OpenAI announced Jalapeño, a custom chip built with Broadcom designed to run inference—the part where models actually answer your questions. It joins Google, Apple, and SpaceX in making hardware to reduce dependence on Nvidia, which has dominated AI chips for years.
stories.why_it_matters: Big AI companies are betting they can build cheaper, faster chips than buying off the shelf.
stories.original_headline: OpenAI’s Jalapeño chip is Big Tech’s spiciest move away from Nvidia

stories.headline: Google made Gemini Nano run twice as fast on Pixel phones
stories.blurb: Google Research found a way to make on-device AI generate text twice as fast by predicting multiple words at once instead of one at a time. The technique, called frozen Multi-Token Prediction, works without retraining the entire model — just adding a small prediction layer on top.
stories.why_it_matters: On-device models can now run faster without burning more battery or needing new hardware.
stories.original_headline: Accelerating Gemini Nano models on Pixel with frozen Multi-Token Prediction

stories.headline: Ex-Nvidia engineers built a robot that can actually do intern work
stories.blurb: Flexion Robotics trained a humanoid robot to handle real office tasks—filing, organizing, basic computer work—by having it learn from watching humans do the job first. The startup's approach skips complex programming and lets the robot pick up tasks the way a new hire would.
stories.why_it_matters: Physical AI that learns by observation could automate entry-level work faster than anyone expected.
stories.original_headline: This Humanoid Robot Is a Terrifyingly Competent Office Intern

stories.headline: Samsung and SK Hynix commit $880 billion to Korea's AI chip push
stories.blurb: South Korea announced a 10-year, $880 billion investment plan from Samsung, SK Hynix, and other companies to build chips and data centers. The government is calling the infrastructure spending essential to compete in the AI era.
stories.why_it_matters: Korea is betting nearly a trillion dollars that chip production and data centers will define AI leadership.
stories.original_headline: Samsung, SK to Spend $880 Billion to Drive Korea’s AI Lead

stories.headline: OpenAI just hired Uber's India chief to run its biggest market outside the US
stories.blurb: OpenAI brought on Uber India's former head to lead operations in India, which has become the company's largest market after the United States. The move is part of a broader push that includes new offices, partnerships, and aggressive hiring across the country.
stories.why_it_matters: India is now OpenAI's second-biggest market globally, and they're staffing up to match.
stories.original_headline: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US

try_this_prompt.title: The skill decay detector
try_this_prompt.prompt: I want to get better at something I'll describe below, but I'm not sure what actually needs work. Tell me: the one skill that's probably degraded without me noticing, the blind spot that comes from doing this on autopilot, and a 10-minute drill I can do this week to diagnose where I really stand.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: When you've been doing something long enough to forget what good looks like.

daily_question: If you could skip one step in your daily workflow by reading your mind, what would it be?
